title: From MCP to Scale: Pipelines That Build Themselves — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
summary: Rafael Levi demonstrates how agents using MCP can inspect websites, generate production scrapers, and autonomously maintain scraping pipelines when sites change. The approach shifts from manual one-off extraction to agent-driven parser generation and repair without human intervention.
writeup: Rafael Levi of Bright Data shows how AI agents use the Model Context Protocol to inspect websites and generate production scrapers automatically, then keep the resulting pipelines running as sites change. The talk frames a shift away from manually written scrapers toward agents that build parsers, optimize token usage, and repair broken collection jobs without human intervention. The practical takeaway: MCP serves as the tool-use layer that lets agents both author and maintain data pipelines, with self-repair handling site changes that would otherwise require manual fixes.
